divergence_notes: Structure: hosted (jayyhk) packs all five sub-questions into one conjunction theorem erdos_353; draft follows FC house style with headline erdos_353 = the leading isosceles-trapezoid question as answer(True) <-> ..., plus four variants (isosceles_triangle, right_angled_triangle, cyclic_quadrilateral, congruent_sides).

Triangle hypotheses: hosted sub-claims (2) and (3) state Koizumi's stronger theorem (MeasurableSet S, 0 < volume S, not Bornology.IsBounded S); draft states the problem as posed (volume A = ⊤, infinite measure). Infinite measure implies positive measure and unboundedness, so hosted is a strengthening; the draft is the faithful problem statement.

Area vocabulary: hosted uses the shoelace formula on the ordered vertex tuple (quadArea / area2); draft uses volume (convexHull ℝ {..}) (Lebesgue measure of the hull). The two agree for vertices in convex position. Hosted's IsoTrapArea1 requires only pairwise distinctness, not convexity, so crossed orderings with |shoelace| = 1 are not excluded there; the draft's IsCcwConvexPolygon requirement excludes them.

Trapezoid definition: hosted = shoelace area 1 + coordinate cross-product parallelism (B-A)x(C-D)=0 + equal legs + equal diagonals + 6 distinctness conjuncts; draft = strictly convex ccw position (IsCcwConvexPolygon ![a,b,c,d]) + affine parallelism line[ℝ,a,b] ∥ line[ℝ,c,d] + equal diagonals. Equal legs is omitted in the draft since it follows from parallel bases + equal diagonals in a convex quadrilateral. Both characterizations rule out non-rectangular parallelograms (rectangles qualify, as isosceles trapezoids).

Right angle: hosted via inner products (<B-A, C-A> = 0, etc.); draft via undirected angle (∠ b a c = π/2, etc.). Equivalent on non-degenerate triangles; in both formalizations degenerate junk values are excluded by the area-1 condition, so neither needs a separate non-degeneracy hypothesis.

Cyclic quadrilateral: hosted UnitCyclicQuad = Concyclic4 (common circle of positive radius) + ConvexQuadCCW + shoelace area 1 on named points; draft = ∃ Q ⊆ A, Q.ncard = 4 ∧ Cospherical Q ∧ volume (convexHull ℝ Q) = 1 (FC 213 style). Four distinct concyclic points are automatically in convex position, so both capture 'the quadrilateral determined by four distinct points on a circle'; Mathlib's Cospherical does not itself demand positive radius, but four distinct points force it.

Congruent-sides polygon: hosted quantifies over C : ZMod n -> Pt with 3 <= n, strict convexity via cross-positivity of cyclically adjacent edges, and ∃ a > 0 common side length; draft quantifies over v : Fin (n+3) -> ℝ² with FC's IsCcwConvexPolygon and all sides equal to the first side (dist (v i) (v (i+1)) = dist (v 0) (v 1), wrap-around Fin addition). Draft additionally requires the counterexample set to be MeasurableSet (the problem quantifies over measurable sets; hosted sub-claim (5) omits measurability).

Negative answer form: both hosted and draft render the congruent-sides answer in the strong KoPr24 form (every such polygon has area < 1) rather than the bare negation (no such polygon of area exactly 1); this matches the solution sentence quoted on the problem page.

Proof status: hosted is a fully proved theorem with no answer() wrapper; draft is an FC statement draft with sorry bodies, answer(True) on the headline, and no proof-link attribute (link_allowed = false).
